hook: One DFM rule change can cut your PCB respin rate by 60%. This is that rule.
headline: Stop Routing Traces at 90-Degree Angles. Your Yield Depends on It.
body: Sharp trace angles under 90 degrees trap etchant during fabrication. This over-etches copper and raises your defect rate by 5-10%. Route at 45 degrees or use curved transitions instead.
A 0.3mm drill with a 0.4mm pad leaves only 0.05mm annular ring -- too thin for reliable plating. Always aim for 0.1mm minimum annular ring to avoid barrel cracks and open circuits.

Run DFM checks early and often, not just before tapeout. Updated 2026 DFM rules now emphasize automation compatibility: pick-and-place machines are faster than ever, but they demand cleaner layouts, consistent component orientation, and standard footprints like 0402 and QFN.
insight: Engage your fab partner when you have your first layout draft -- not your final Gerber. Early DFM review catches 80% of issues that cause costly respins.
source: Sierra Circuits / Sierra Assembly / NCAB Group
caption:
Three DFM rules. Zero excuses.

Route at 45 degrees. Keep annular rings above 0.1mm. Run DFM checks before your first review, not after your last respin.

Simple changes. Massive yield improvements.

#DFM #PCBDesign #DesignForManufacturing #PCBTips #ElectronicsEngineering #HardwareDesign
